characters.name: tent
characters.description: The tatty tent that bob lives in outside of Waitrose

config.page_text:
Camping

The story of two people from different worlds...

badcode

media.prompt: the exact building and lighting from [1] but zoomed into the window like we have done in the previous image

config.text:
The City

Another day at the office.

Only winners are allowed to work in this building...

animation.transition_prompt:
We zoom in gradually from the first frame to the last frame keeping focus the entire time on the lit office floor in the final frame.

We should never loose focus on the lit office floor in the final frame.

text_bubbles.text: Let's circle back to synergise our bandwidth…

config.text:
And the winner is...

Tarquin - he just closed another deal and is feeling very pleased with himself.

The shareholders are happy, the spreadsheet is locked in, he is clearly better at his job than everyone else in the room...

text_bubbles.text:
Tarquin, as per, you've done it again, old boy.

How do you do it?

Charles, it's easy!


You just have to have a "Winning Mentality" (tm)
He is so cool...
What are you up to this weekend mate?

I'm off to an ayahuasca retreat in Wales!

There are parts of my personality I am keen to get to know more.
He is so cool...
What are you up to this weekend mate?

I'm off to an ayahuasca retreat in Wales!

There are parts of my personality I am keen to get to know more.
Another week of impressing these morons, a chimp could do what they do. This retreat cannot come soon enough. When will this end? Why is it always up to me?

media.prompt: Move the small green-grey camping tent from the center of the parking lot up close to the Waitrose store entrance, positioned just a few spaces back from the covered walkway area. The tent should be set up in an empty parking space near the shop front, with its door facing away from the viewer (toward the store), so we see only the back and sides of the tent. Directly opposite the tent's unseen door, place the [wank tank] facing it across a gap of empty parking spaces. Surround the tent and [wank tank] with a cluster of clearly empty parking bays, creating an island of vacant tarmac near the store while the rest of the lot remains filled with parked cars as before. Keep the two figures — one in a bright yellow raincoat and one holding a dark umbrella pushing a shopping cart — walking nearby. Maintain the exact same elevated drone-angle composition looking down toward the Waitrose storefront, the same overcast grey sky, wet reflective asphalt, misty rain atmosphere, and dim warm parking lot lamps. Shot on 35mm film with fine natural grain, subtle dust specks, and gentle gate weave. Muted cool-neutral color palette with low saturation, soft contrast, lifted blacks, and gentle highlight roll-off on the store's interior glow and lamp halos. Vintage lens softness with mild halation around the practical lights, naturalistic motivated lighting, calm observational documentary tone, landscape orientation.

config.text:
Parking

The next day, Tarquin arrives at the Waitrose car park.

He parks in the only way he knows how...

animation.transition_prompt: Elevated wide shot of a rain-soaked Waitrose supermarket car park on an overcast day. A black BMW SUV enters from the bottom-left of frame, driving at moderate speed along the wet tarmac aisle between parked cars, its headlights casting long reflections across the glistening surface. The vehicle travels forward toward a blue camping tent pitched incongruously in an empty parking bay beside the shopping trolley shelter. The camera holds nearly static with only the subtlest drift rightward and slightly downward, tracking the car's arrival. As the BMW approaches the tent, it brakes hard and abruptly — the vehicle's nose dips noticeably under the forward weight transfer of heavy deceleration, suspension compressing at the front. Vivid red brake lights flare to life, their glow reflecting sharply off the wet black asphalt in streaked crimson pools beneath and behind the car. The SUV comes to a sudden, decisive halt just in front of the tent, engine idling. Steady light rain continues falling throughout, creating subtle ripples on puddles and maintaining the slick sheen across the entire parking lot surface. Overcast diffused daylight, muted color palette with the green Waitrose signage glowing warmly in the background. Cinematic documentary tone, handheld stillness with natural micro-movements.

Parking

media.prompt: exactly like the previous image but rotate the car to it's left by 45 degrees

text_bubbles.text: Why make my day worse than it has to be…

config.text: Bob, a homeless man, spots Tarquin's black 4x4; it's safe to say he is not a fan.

animation.transition_prompt: Elevated wide shot of a rain-soaked Waitrose supermarket car park on an overcast day. The black SUV sits parked with its rear brake lights glowing red, reflecting off the wet tarmac. The driver's door slowly opens and a man in a dark leather jacket and jeans steps out, carrying a canvas bag in his right hand. He plants his feet on the glistening wet ground, closes the door behind him, and takes a few deliberate steps toward the rear of the vehicle before pausing mid-stride. He turns his body to the right, directing his gaze toward the blue camping tent pitched absurdly in the adjacent parking space. His posture shifts from casual to puzzled as he faces the tent. Throughout, steady light rain continues to fall, fine droplets visible against the dark asphalt, with puddles shimmering beneath the diffused grey daylight. The blue tent fabric ripples subtly in a gentle breeze. Camera holds static from the elevated angle, maintaining the wide framing of the surreal scene.

media.prompt: Just like the image in [1] but [tarquin] is stood outside looking at the [tent]

reference_images.description: The wank tank parked outside the tent

text_bubbles.text: Thanks for taking up two parking spaces with that charming tattered tent. You should have gotten the tent I purchased; it even has a cigar holder.
Why do people give their hard-earned cash to this putrid rat? He’ll just shoot it up his arm anyway. Get a job, you piece of…

media.prompt: exactly like the previous image but rotate the car to it's left by 45 degrees

text_bubbles.text: Since day one of being here, the guilt of being in the way of real people never fades. That being said, at least acknowledge that we are both being rained on by the same shitty weather; you are not blind to the elements that we cannot control.

config.text: Bob stares into traffic all day, judging the shit out of them, that’s all that there is to do.

media.prompt: exactly like the previous image but rotate the car to it's left by 45 degrees

text_bubbles.text: Luck, simulation theory or God, whatever your poison, mine is down the gullet of this bottleneck, it causes the interminable amount of throbbing guilt to fade to black. Plus, it's gluten-free.

config.text: Bob stares into traffic all day, judging the shit out of them, that’s all that there is to do.

text_bubbles.text: I remember 2008 like it was yesterday, the day of the car crash, I miss my wife so much I… I… I shudder every time I see those stupid wank tanks. Her face, those lifeless eyes… God let me forget these horrors. Amen.

config.text: Bob stares into traffic all day, judging the shit out of them, that’s all that there is to do.

Wales

The next day, Tarquin heads off to Gwydir Forest in Wales, you know, the most famous place in the world to do Ayahuasca ceremonies. He’s probably got 8 grand in speeding fines.

Wales

The next day, Tarquin heads off to Gwydir Forest in Wales, you know, the most famous place in the world to do Ayahuasca ceremonies. He’s probably got 8 grand in speeding fines.

animation.transition_prompt: Elevated drone shot, static camera position, looking down a wet two-lane road cutting through dense dark evergreen forest on an overcast, foggy day. A dark-colored car emerges from the misty curve in the middle distance, traveling toward the camera at approximately 40-50 km/h along the right lane. The car's warm yellow headlights switch on and cast elongated reflections across the rain-slicked asphalt, the light spreading and intensifying on the wet surface as the vehicle draws closer. Fine mist drifts slowly left to right through the tree canopy. The tall spruce and pine trees sway almost imperceptibly in a light breeze, with subtle movement concentrated in the uppermost branches. The overcast cloud layer shifts very gently overhead. Tiny dark specks of debris or rain particles drift slowly through the air. The road markings, white dashed center line and solid edge lines, remain fixed. The roadside guide posts and triangular warning sign stay stationary. The forest maintains its deep muted green-grey color palette throughout. Moody, cinematic atmosphere with desaturated cool tones, shallow atmospheric perspective from fog reducing visibility beyond 200 meters. Smooth, continuous forward motion of the car from the foggy bend to the lower-center of frame.

config.text:
Wales

The next day, Tarquin heads off to Gwydir Forest in Wales, you know, the most famous place in the world to do Ayahuasca ceremonies. He’s probably got 8 grand in speeding fines.

Tarquin is thrilled by the presence of white people with dreadlocks, crystals, and chanting. To escape this, you would need the most powerful psychedelic known to man. Or what they call “plant medicine” or Ayahuasca or something.

text_bubbles.text: I'm Moonwhisper Ascending. I am here to cleanse my soul, also to do research for my new book called "The Wound That Teaches."

config.text: Tarquin is thrilled by the presence of white people with dreadlocks, crystals, and chanting. To escape this, you would need the most powerful psychedelic known to man. Or what they call “plant medicine” or Ayahuasca or something.

text_bubbles.text: I should have brought Daddy’s hunting rifle…

config.text: Tarquin is thrilled by the presence of white people with dreadlocks, crystals, and chanting. To escape this, you would need the most powerful psychedelic known to man. Or what they call “plant medicine” or Ayahuasca or something.

text_bubbles.text: Fuck me, that kicked in quick!

animation.error_message: failed to extract frames: ffmpeg failed: exec: "ffmpeg": executable file not found in $PATH, stderr:

text_bubbles.text: Tarquin, I've come back here to warn you, you must change your ways, there is no time to fuck around, AI is coming, you fool! Unburden yourself from the judgment of your elbow-patch afflicted peers and take action. For our sake!
I came here to learn more about myself, not this nonsensical bullshit. Why have I lowered myself to such squalor?

config.text: Tarquin snaps out of it and heads back to his tent to lie down. He resents the shaman for giving him the drug he asked for...

pages.name: future car park drone shot vid

text_bubbles.text: Ouch! The fuck is happening...

config.text: Tarquin, disoriented and confused, steps out of the tent and immediately falls on his arse.

text_bubbles.text: The lake... the trip... it all...
I've never been this cold. How do people live like this? It's preposterous!

config.text: Tarquin goes to warm his hands, looks like he needs a minute.

text_bubbles.text: Cold ain't it? What you doing here, havn't seen you in some time.
I'm frozen, wish I came more prepared. Look I really don't know what to do, probably for the first time in my life... um whatever your name is... cough... sir can you...
Bob, my name's Bob mate, I'm not sure what I can, in fact, do for you, really, unless you're hiding beer behind your silver spoon. On second thoughts, keep that one bud, looks like you need it.
Bob, please, I am literally begging you. Oh, no offence there, old boy, I am not quite used to lowering myself to such...

Slow down, mate, don't make too much noise. If you kick off, I won't be able to stop these lot. Just don't show weakness like that; they can smell it a mile away. Sleep with both eyes open, take inventory, and keep your head down. Also, sleep with a blade under your pillow, this ain't Eton.
Well, what do I do now then?
Crazy bastards...

config.text: Tarquin sprints into the forest next to the car park, things are looking up.
He gives up trying to start a fire after remembering he had never done that before.

text_bubbles.text: What was that?

config.text: Startled by the frustrated screams of a woman in the distance, he decides to investigate. Maybe he thinks he can use his newfound survival skills to help out.

text_bubbles.text: Start shovelling or start fucking off!

config.text: Startled by the frustrated screams of a woman in the distance, he decides to investigate. Maybe he thinks he can use his newfound survival skills to help out.

They don't say much to each other, just a mutual understanding, a subtext you cannot hold firm, one you earn in the weirdest of ways. It takes them all night to bury the corpse. She didn't say who it was.
Tarquin scrounges for food by the bins, maybe there will be his favourite brand of crouton.
Tarquin sits down in his tent and takes a big swig; that is all there is left to do.
With his hands being as cold and numb as his mind, he finds an old hotel. With no real plan, he heads inside.
Looking at the dusty bottle of scotch like it's the holy grail. The alcohol gives the illusion of warmth, a welcomed substute. He puts on the headphones; Badcode starts playing...
Tarquin sits down in his tent and takes a big swig; that is all there is left to do.

config.widget_text:
## The End

please follow for more

settings.style:
35mm FILM STYLE — STRICT
Late-70s/early-80s photochemical cinema aesthetic. Scanned 35mm negative with fine grain, subtle dust/specks, gentle gate weave.
Color: Muted, cool-neutral, low saturation, soft contrast, gentle highlight roll-off, lifted blacks.
Optics: Vintage lens softness, mild halation on practicals, no clinical sharpness.
Lighting: Naturalistic, motivated by practicals, no HDR sheen.
Composition: Calm, observational, documentary-real tone.
HARD RULES:

ALWAYS landscape orientation (never portrait)
No hyperreal/HDR, no CGI sharpness, no neon/cyberpunk, no painterly/anime styles, no text/logos/watermarks
